dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3357 IS: Social Security Survivor Benefits Equity Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Welch (for himself, Mr. Sanders, Ms. Warren, Mr. Whitehouse, Mr. Blumenthal, Mr. Booker, and Mr. Markey) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance

form.official-title: To amend title II of the Social Security Act to update the amount of Social Security lump sum death payments and index lump sum death payments to inflation.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Social Security Survivor Benefits Equity Act.

section.header: Updating and indexing the amount of the Social Security lump sum death payment

subsection.text: Section 202(i) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 402(i)) is amended, in the matter preceding paragraph (1), by striking three times and all that follows through smaller, and inserting $2,900 (or, in the case of such an insured individual who dies in a calendar year after 2026, an amount equal to $2,900 increased by the percentage increase in the Consumer Price Index for Urban Wage Earners and Clerical Workers for the period that begins with October 2025 and ends with October of the calendar year preceding the calendar year involved and rounded to the nearest multiple of $10).

The amendment made by this section shall take effect on January 1, 2026, and shall apply to lump sum death payments payable under section 202(i) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 402(i)) with respect to individuals who die on or after such date.
